item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this one, but my biggest problem is the word choice/phrasing in some of the paragraphs.

+ show block
– hide block

contained at the center of a six-meter-diameter refrigerated containment cell Personally, I think “Contained in a refrigerated containment cell 6 meters in diameter” sounds better

are to be removed by a member of D-Class personnel following each Event I think should be “removed by D-Class personnel following each event."

Any minor interaction with an area of this new skin (including slight changes in temperature or air pressure) I don’t think the work minor is needed here, or the parenthesis. Maybe “Any physical or environmental change or interaction with the newly created epidermis"

reported hearing the sounds of cutlery, laughter, and muffled talking Minor nitpick, but I think “muffled speech” is better

the minimum wage offered to Miller & Carter's Steakhouse employees. I think it’d just be better to say “the wage offered by SCP-4788’s previous place of employment.”

I also believe the last paragraph is a bit superfluous, as it doesn’t really add depth to anything.

Overall, I enjoyed it. The above are only small problems. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A little too on-the-nose for my taste. Still gets a +1 from me, but only barely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God I absolutely love this, great imagery.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Novote for now, but I definitely see potential in the concept. The steak thing killed it for me, but I don't know why. Maybe it's because the vagueness of it seems less purposeful and more like you weren't sure where you were going with it. Still, I have a soft spot for this SCP, so I'll be keeping an eye on this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like I've read this conceit before, but done subtler, done more interestingly. I'm not feeling it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: novote for now. idk, this seems like body horror for the sake of body horror to me. but i feel like there is something i'm missing on this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This makes me feel the kind of uncomfortable that I like. Well done especially in this much space; +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One thing I like to do with new SCPs is read through the containment procedures and try to imagine the anomaly based on that information. The challenge was great for this one. +1, would order the steak again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's interesting body horror. But i mostly dig the bureaucratic part of this one. I'm currently tending towards upvote.

Some nitpicks:

  • Taxidermy removes all muscles from the body, you should mention the corpse still has it's muscles before they start spasming.
  • The clinical tone seems somewhat… pretentious. I'm still new to the site so this migth be void, but i tend to apply the "can an office clerk use this guidelines in a hassle"-guideline.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
^ that is disgusting. But also cleverly written, and I like it!
(+1)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is disgusting and I dont like this. +1

item.title: Moo
item.content:encoded:
reserved

Wrote this for a challenge, to get the tag golf "bovine & cadaver". In retrospect, I don't actually like it enough to do a proper commentary.
